title: Derris uliginosa
taxonomy: Derris uliginosa belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread in the plant kingdom, comprising over 19,000 species. The genus Derris is a group of tropical plants, often found in wet and humid environments. The classification of Derris uliginosa is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its compound leaves and white or pink flowers. By examining its botanical features, we can place Derris uliginosa within the broader taxonomic hierarchy of the plant kingdom.
uses: Derris uliginosa is a plant that has been found to have various medicinal properties. However, the available information on its specific uses is limited. The plant contains rotenone, a toxic compound that has been used in traditional medicine for its antiparasitic and antiseptic properties. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses of Derris uliginosa. Traditional healers have reportedly used the plant to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
benefits: Derris uliginosa is known to have various health benefits due to its active compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. constituents: Derris uliginosa contains a range of active constituents, including rotenone and derrisine, which have been identified as key compounds responsible for its biological activities. These compounds have been found to exhibit insecticidal and acaricidal properties. Additionally, the plant contains other bioactive molecules such as flavonoids and terpenoids. parts: Derris uliginosa is a plant species within the pea family, Fabaceae. Derris uliginosa contains compounds that have been used in traditional medicine, including the roots and other plant parts. The medicinal properties of Derris uliginosa are not as well-studied as those of its close relative, Derris elliptica, which contains rotenone, a potent insecticide and fish poison. However, Derris uliginosa is also known to contain rotenone and related compounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties of Derris uliginosa.
preparations: Derris uliginosa is not commonly discussed in herbal contexts. Derris uliginosa is a plant that can be found in certain regions of South America and is not typically used in herbal preparations. side_effects: Derris uliginosa is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but like any plant-based remedy, it may have side effects. Derris uliginosa contains compounds that can interact with certain medications and may cause allergic reactions in some individuals. Common side effects of Derris uliginosa may include nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset.
